exemplar.title: What constraints are there in the pursuit of knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: What constraints are there in the pursuit of knowledge?

strengths: Clear identification of three distinct real-world objects with authentic contexts (telescope, tweet, historical photograph).
Consistent links to the chosen prompt (âWhat constraints are there in the pursuit of knowledge?â) across all objects, each tied convincingly to technology themes.
Use of concrete evidence (e.g., telescope cost figures, algorithm explanations, historical facts about Turing) and citations demonstrating research effort.
Focused theme (âKnowledge and Technologyâ) that frames the exhibition effectively and provides coherence.
weaknesses: Occasional lack of precision in explanationsâfor example, the justified true belief discussion drifts into TOK theory without tightly analysing the constraint.
Repetition and depth issues in Object 2âs algorithmic echo-chamber analysis, without probing deeper epistemic implications.
Conceptual ambiguity in treating Alan Turing himself as an âobjectâ and unsubstantiated causal claims about the impact of his death on AI progress.
Inconsistent citation formatting and some redundancy in the concluding justification, limiting the overall polish and clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition aligns well with the 5â6 band descriptor: it identifies three objects with real-world contexts, explains and justifies their relevance to the prompt, and supports many points with evidence and citation. However, it falls short of the 7â8 band due to imprecision in some links to TOK theory, repetition in analysis, conceptual ambiguity around Object 3, and inconsistent referencing. Awarding 6 reflects the workâs clear strengths while acknowledging the depth, precision, and clarity needed for higher bands.

annotations.comment: The prompt is stated clearly at the outset, providing a focused question that frames the entire exhibition.
The theme âKnowledge and Technologyâ is well chosen and aligns strongly with the promptâs focus on constraints in the pursuit of knowledge.
The definition of knowledge as strictly âjustified true beliefâ is too simplistic; consider acknowledging Gettier-style counterexamples to deepen your conceptual grounding.
The telescope is introduced with rich contextâmodel specifics and purchase costâgiving a compelling realâworld anchor for the first object.
Consider adding metadata (e.g., date, location of observation) to the telescope image to reinforce its authenticity and realâworld context.
The budget comparison between entryâlevel and advanced telescopes is useful, but you could strengthen it with a brief costâbenefit analysis or market price data.
The screenshot would benefit from a visible timestamp or URL bar to confirm when and where the tweet was accessed.
The assertion that Turingâs death directly halted AI progress is compelling but remains unsubstantiated; include historical sources or expert commentary.
The connection between societal homophobia and constraint on Turingâs research is well articulated and directly ties to the prompt.
Incorporate scholarly literature on discriminationâs impact on scientific careers to reinforce the example of social values constraining knowledge.
The description of light pollution effectively illustrates an environmental constraint on evidence gathering.
The analysis drifts into general TOK terminology without precisely linking how limited evidence prevents a justified true belief in this context.
To deepen the link to the prompt, explicitly reference which element of the JTB model is most compromised by limited technology.
Link back to âKnowledge and Technologyâ by discussing how advances in optical tech could alleviate this specific constraint.
The tweet is introduced with clear attribution, providing a strong realâworld context for Object 2.

The description of Armstrongâs refusal remains largely descriptive; a deeper epistemic critique of how misinformation spreads via authority figures would strengthen your link to knowledge constraints.
Consider citing specific studies on socialâmedia algorithms to illustrate exactly how content filtering limits exposure to dissenting perspectives.
The paragraph on echo chambers cogently explains how algorithmic filtering constrains knowledge acquisition.
You could bolster this claim by including data on engagement rates or user studies showing how often echo chambers exclude opposing views.
The image grouping for Object 3 lacks explicit linkage to the values discussion; clarify whether the photograph itself is the exhibit or Turing as a figure.
Referring to Alan Turing himself as an âobjectâ raises conceptual concerns; consider framing the photograph or his archived work as the object instead.
The concluding justification repeats earlier analysis; consider consolidating to eliminate redundancy and sharpen impact.
Citation styles vary between Reuters, Harvard Gazette, and Twitter sources; standardize formatting (e.g., APA) for consistency.
For the tweet citation, include the full URL and retrieval date to satisfy rigorous referencing standards.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
